intro: Python manipulates collections sequentially using procedural logic or object-relational mapping patterns. SQL is a declarative query language designed to extract, transform, and manage relational database records. Developers rewrite data logic from Python into SQL to run queries directly inside the database engine, eliminating network latency and maximizing query performance.

diffs.from: Imperative/procedural; you write the explicit steps to process data.
diffs.to: Declarative; you specify the desired output criteria, and the engine optimizes execution.

diffs.from: In-memory object graphs, dynamic structures, and arrays.
diffs.to: Relational tables structured into strict schemas of rows and columns.

diffs.from: Explicit loops (for, while) or functional collection comprehensions.
diffs.to: Set-based relational algebra operations (JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY).

diffs.from: Transient in-memory execution states that reset when the script ends.
diffs.to: Persistent ACID-compliant transaction states written directly to storage disks.

diffs.from: Developer-optimized loops, reference tracking, and algorithmic choices.
diffs.to: Query planner evaluations using internal indices and structural analytics.

examples.title: Filter and Aggregate Data
examples.description: Filtering data entries and calculating a sum based on a grouping key.

examples.title: Join Datasets
examples.description: Combining records from two distinct sources based on a shared tracking key.

tips.title: Think in Sets
tips.body: Avoid processing rows with procedural loops; use set-based filters and relational joins instead.

tips.title: Utilize Database Indexes
tips.body: Ensure columns used frequently in WHERE and JOIN clauses are indexed to avoid full table scans.

tips.title: Leverage Aggregate Functions
tips.body: Offload calculations like SUM, AVG, and COUNT to the database engine instead of computing them in application memory.

tips.title: Use Prepared Statements
tips.body: Always parameterize dynamic inputs to protect database systems against SQL injection attacks.
